revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
Text replacement - "↵[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects W]]" to " <noinclude>[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects W]]</noinclude>"
Bot: remove deleted category Automatic wikipedia template redirects W
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 4 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23693191906|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} ''Wbr'' indicates a ''word break'' opportunity. This template includes the HTML tag [https://www.w3.org/TR/html5/text-level-semantics.html#the-wbr-element {{tag|wbr|s}}], which is used to tell the browser of an optional, non-hyphenated line-break. It can be used with: * {{tlx|nbsp}} or the equivalent   (non-breaking space) * {{tlx|nowrap}} or the equivalent {{nowrap|...}} or {{nowrap|...}} (character string that will not break even at a normal space) to control line breaking where this is important. This template can safely be [[WP:SUBST|substituted]], though there is no particular reason to do so. For breaking up long words, use {{tlx|soft hyphen}} instead. == Usage == Use is simple; there are no parameters: * {{tlx|wbr}} or * {{tlxs|wbr}} === Controlling line-breaking in infoboxes === This template can be used to cause an indentation after a linebreak, in [[Template:Plainlist|unbulleted lists]] inside [[WP:Infobox|infoboxes]] and in [[WP:Table|tables]] with constrained cell widths, by using {{nowrap|{{wbr}} }} between words in an entry, so that when they wrap they are not mistaken for separate entries. See example infobox to the side. Example infobox (rendered to the side of the example code): {{Infobox film| |name=Test Infobox II: The Revenge |... |starring={{Plainlist| * Jane Smith * {{nobr|Johannes-Friedrich}}{{wbr}} Zauberzunge{{wbr}} {{nobr|von der Hasenpfeffer}} * John Garcia }} |... |italic_title=no }} {{Infobox film| |name=Test Infobox II: The Revenge |... |starring={{Plainlist| * Jane Smith * {{nobr|Johannes-Friedrich}}{{wbr}} Zauberzunge{{wbr}} {{nobr|von der Hasenpfeffer}} * John Garcia }} |... }} === Breaking up slashes === {{see|MOS:SLASH}} When there are long words on both sides of a slash (solidus), this template may be used to induce a line break after the slash. Some browsers allow optional breaking after a slash even without this. : installation/{{wbr}}execution : installation/{{wbr}}execution {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Line-handling templates]] [[Category:Inline spacing templates]] [[Category:Character templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24999656626|time triggered pipeline]]
